name: Judy Garland
description: American actress, singer, dancer and vaudevillian (1922–1969)
abstract: Judy Garland (born Frances Ethel Gumm; June 10, 1922 – June 22, 1969) was an American actress, singer, and vaudevillian whose career spanned four decades. She is known for her artistic range and strong contralto voice, working in a variety of genres including musicals, comedies, and dramas.

works.t: I Can't Give You Anything But Love
A Damsel in Distress: A Foggy Day
When You're Smiling (The Whole World Smiles With You)
Zing! Went the Strings of My Heart
The Wizard of Oz: Over the Rainbow
Brigadoon: Almost Like Being in Love

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: I called Judy Garland, who was my close friend out here—she was like a little sister—
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: I actually spoke to Judy Garland. She said, “What’s this for? Is this for a newspaper, or a magazine, or what?” I said, “It’s just my personal collection.” “Oh, okay.” Just that moment of speaking to Judy Garland, that was a big thing with me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Skitch Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: And their second trip out was Denver, where their pianist became ill... asked if I could be sent down to help them out... And a day later here I am on the road for MGM.
about_quotes.doc_title: Skitch Henderson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Milford, Connecticut, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: John W. Bubbles
about_quotes.evidence: I danced, I did my act and I did a number with Judy Garland called -- what's the name of that song? 'Me and My Shadow,' is it? Yeah, 'Me and My Shadow' I think we did.

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: I don’t think many of the young people or older people may not remember the name Joe Pasternak. He was the biggest producer of musicals. Judy Garland and Mickey Rooney. All those musicals he produced.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Diblasio
about_quotes.evidence: my parents took me to see the Basie band with Judy Garland at the Manhattan Music Center... But anyway so she sings “Over the Rainbow” with Basie’s band. So I heard that.
